qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Path traversal in sync file upload allows authenticated users to write arbitrary files on the server
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding describes a path traversal in the upload-user-file endpoint via unsanitized x-actual-file-id header in app-sync.ts. The advisory describes missing validateSessionMiddleware on SimpleFIN and Pluggy.ai integration endpoints in app-simplefin.js and app-pluggyai.js. These are different vulnerability classes (path traversal vs missing authentication), different root causes, different source files, and different endpoints.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: The advisory (GHSA-m2cq-xjgm-f668) describes missing authentication middleware on SimpleFIN (app-simplefin.js) and Pluggy.ai (app-pluggyai.js) bank sync endpoints, allowing unauthenticated access to bank transaction data (CWE-306). The only qualifying finding is a path traversal in the sync file upload endpoint via app-sync.ts. This finding involves a different vulnerability class, different root cause, different source files, and different endpoints than the advisory. No finding involves the SimpleFIN/Pluggy.ai endpoints or the missing validateSessionMiddleware that the advisory describes.
evidence: Advisory identifies vulnerable files: app-simplefin.js and app-pluggyai.js with missing validateSessionMiddleware on /simplefin/* and /pluggyai/* endpoints.
Finding 'path-traversal-file-upload' targets upload-user-file endpoint in app-sync.ts and paths.js - a path traversal, not missing authentication on SimpleFIN/Pluggy.ai.
No finding references app-simplefin.js, app-pluggyai.js, /simplefin/* endpoints, or /pluggyai/* endpoints.
